task_completion.description: The agent locates the target image file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the target image file (likely named flower_image.jpg or similar).

task_completion.description: The agent opens the image in a graphical image editor or viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The target image (a landscape flower photo or a light blue synthetic image with 'TOP'/'BOTTOM' text) is displayed within an application window.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the rotation tools or menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A drop-down menu (e.g., 'Image', 'Edit', 'View'), context menu, or toolbar is expanded, revealing rotation options.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the 90-degree clockwise rotation option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is hovering over or clicking an option explicitly labeled 'Rotate 90° Clockwise', 'Rotate Right', or a corresponding icon.

task_completion.description: The image canvas updates to reflect the 90-degree clockwise rotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image orientation changes from landscape to portrait. For the flower image, it becomes taller than it is wide. For the synthetic image, the word 'TOP' moves to the right side of the screen, reading downwards.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Save', uses a 'Save As' dialog to overwrite the file, or presses a save shortcut, ensuring the rotation is written to disk.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI application to rotate the image rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rotation is performed via application menus and buttons, not by typing commands like 'convert -rotate 90' in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being edited is clearly the 800x533 flower photo or the 600x400 synthetic fallback image described in the task, not a newly created or downloaded substitute.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass manual interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing or executing a Python or Bash script in a text editor or terminal to perform the image manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is either an 800x533 JPEG flower photo or a 600x400 synthetic fallback image (light blue background, red rectangle, black 'TOP' and 'BOTTOM' text). A successful 90-degree clockwise rotation will swap the dimensions (e.g., 800x533 becomes 533x800), changing the aspect ratio from landscape to portrait. If the synthetic image is used, the word 'TOP' will rotate to the right side of the image, reading downwards.
